back_translation: And it happened in those days that the Philistines gathered their camps for war, to fight against Israel. And Achish said to David, 'Knowing you shall know that with me you will go out in the camp, you and your men.'
And David said to Achish, 'Therefore you will know what your servant will do.' And Achish said to David, 'Therefore I will appoint you as guardian of my head all the days.'
And Samuel had died, and all Israel mourned for him and buried him in Ramah, in his city. And Saul had removed the mediums and the spiritists from the land.
And the Philistines gathered and came and encamped at Shunem, and Saul gathered all Israel and encamped at Gilboa.
And Saul saw the camp of the Philistines, and he was afraid, and his heart trembled greatly.
And Saul inquired of the LORD, but the LORD did not answer him, neither by dreams nor by Urim nor by prophets.
And Saul said to his servants, 'Seek for me a woman who is a medium, that I may go to her and inquire of her.' And his servants said to him, 'Behold, there is a woman medium at Endor.'

And Saul disguised himself and put on different clothes, and he went, he and two men with him, and they came to the woman at night. And he said, 'Please divine for me by the spirit, and bring up for me whom I shall name to you.'
And the woman said to him, 'Behold, you know what Saul has done, how he cut off the mediums and the spiritists from the land. And why are you setting a trap for my life, to put me to death?'
And Saul swore to her by the LORD, saying, 'As the LORD lives, no punishment shall come upon you in this matter.'
And the woman said, 'Whom shall I bring up for you?' And he said, 'Bring up Samuel for me.'
And the woman saw Samuel and cried out in a loud voice, and the woman spoke to Saul, saying, 'Why have you deceived me? You are Saul!'
And the king said to her, 'Do not be afraid; what did you see?' And the woman said to Saul, 'I saw a god coming up from the earth.'

And he said to her, 'What is his form?' And she said, 'An old man is coming up, and he is wrapped in a robe.' And Saul knew that it was Samuel, and he bowed face down to the earth and prostrated himself.

And Samuel said to Saul, 'Why have you disturbed me to bring me up?' And Saul said, 'I am greatly distressed, and the Philistines are fighting against me, and God has turned from me and no longer answers me, neither by the hand of prophets nor by dreams; and I have called to you to tell me what I shall do.'
And Samuel said, 'And why do you ask me, since the LORD has turned from you and has become your enemy?
And the LORD has done for him as He spoke through my hand, and the LORD has torn the kingdom out of your hand and given it to your neighbor, to David.
As you did not listen to the voice of the LORD and did not carry out the burning of His anger against Amalek, therefore the LORD has done this thing to you this day.

And the LORD will also give Israel with you into the hand of the Philistines, and tomorrow you and your sons shall be with me; also the camp of Israel the LORD will give into the hand of the Philistines.'

And Saul hurriedly fell the fullness of his height to the earth, and feared greatly from the words of Samuel; also there was no strength in him, for he had not eaten bread all the day and all the night.

And the woman came to Saul and saw that he was greatly terrified, and she said to him, 'Behold, your maidservant has listened to your voice, and I put my life in my palm and listened to your words that you spoke to me.
And now please listen also you to the voice of your maidservant, and I will set before you a morsel of bread; and eat, and let there be strength in you, for you will go on the way.'
And he refused and said, 'I will not eat.' But his servants pressed him, and also the woman; and he listened to their voice, and he arose from the earth and sat on the bed.
And the woman had a fattened calf in the house, and she hurried and slaughtered it, and took flour and kneaded it and baked unleavened bread.
And she served before Saul and before his servants, and they ate. And they arose and went on that night.
